title: Meeting the Needs of the Global Quantum Science Community: A Call to Action
why:
- Marginalized quantum scientists report experiencing more hardships and challenges than their more privileged peers across all measured areas.
- Over half of marginalized quantum scientists surveyed have considered leaving the field, compared to very few non-marginalized scientists.
- Marginalized respondents have a significantly lower perception of career opportunities in quantum science compared to non-marginalized respondents.
- Retaining diverse talent and improving equity, diversity, and inclusion (EDI) are identified as essential for a thriving and globally competitive quantum ecosystem.
- The findings are based on the largest international survey to date on diversity, workplace climate, and opportunity in quantum science and technology, with input from over 1,000 participants.
- Current top-down diversity initiatives have not resulted in substantial, measurable change within the quantum science community.
